description: Configure an Apache reverse proxy for the Socioboard backend microservices to route traffic over port 80. The four Node.js microservices are running via pm2 on their default ports: User (3000), Feeds (3001), Publish (3002), and Notification (3003). Map these services to the paths /proxy/user/, /proxy/feeds/, /proxy/publish/, and /proxy/notification/ respectively within the Apache configuration (e.g., /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf). Additionally, update the PHP frontend configuration file at /opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/.env to point the variables API_URL, API_URL_FEEDS, API_URL_PUBLISH, and API_URL_NOTIFICATION to these new proxy endpoints on localhost. A successful configuration allows HTTP requests to the proxy paths on port 80 to reach the underlying Node.js applications.
